channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: CBP phosphorylation maintains intestinal homeostasis by supporting the stem cell niche through versican.
item.description: Nature communications - CREB-binding protein (CBP) is a transcriptional coactivator, and we reported its phosphorylation regulates cell fate. Impaired CBP phosphorylation alters its binding preference for p53 in CBPAA mic...

item.title: The SILVER Platter for Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD): An IBD Pharmacy Technician-Led Subcutaneous Biologic Home Delivery Service Is Associated with Sustained Improvement in Adherence and Active Health Management.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Subcutaneous medications are associated with lower adherence, risking adverse disease outcomes. This project aimed to evaluate the effect of a pharmacy technician-led subcutaneous biologic home del...

item.title: Promoting Self-Care in Individuals with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Scoping Review.
item.description: Journal of gastrointestinal and liver diseases : JGLD - Review - Self-care has emerged as a pivotal component in the long-term management of inflammatory bowel disease, yet the literature remains fragmented regarding educational interventions supporting its impl...

item.title: Dietary inflammatory index and objective disease activity in IBD: no association found.
item.description: European journal of clinical nutrition - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) involves genetic and environmental factors, but the relationship between disease activity, adiposity, and diet remains unclear. To investigate the association betwe...

item.title: The Impact of Informal Caregiving on Patient-Reported Outcomes, Psychological Well-Being and Quality of Life in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review.
item.description: Nursing reports (Pavia, Italy) - Review - Background/Objectives: While caregiver burden in Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is well documented, the association between informal support and patient-reported outcomes (PROs), particularly hea...

item.title: The IBD-FITT Study-Moderate-Intensity Exercise for Patients with Active Inflammatory Bowel Disease: An Open-Label Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background: Exercise has been suggested as a supplementary modality for Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD), but supporting evidence remains scarce. We aimed to assess whether a 12-week physical exerc...
